content.verdict: Sun City Camp plants 50 transparent bubble tents and a panoramic star-gazing dome in the middle of Wadi Rum's Martian sandstone — the same Lawrence of Arabia landscape used as Mars in half the films of the last decade. Honeymooners fall asleep with the entire Milky Way above the bed, no roof in the way.
content.best_room: Book a Martian Tent with panoramic stargazing dome — a clear hemisphere above the king bed pointed north toward the Milky Way, with a private terrace, en-suite bathroom and Bedouin rugs. Traditional black-goat-hair tents are atmospheric but the bubbles are the honeymoon choice every time.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival into Mars
itinerary_7_nights.description: A 4x4 transfer from Wadi Rum village drops you at camp as the red sandstone glows at sunset. Welcome mint tea on the terrace, a zarb dinner cooked under hot sand and first stars from your dome.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Full-day jeep tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Sunrise 4x4 to the Khazali canyon, Nabatean inscriptions, the red sand dune climb and Lawrence's spring. Picnic lunch in the shade of a rock arch and sunset at Um Fruth rock bridge.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Camel caravan and quiet
itinerary_7_nights.description: Two-hour dawn camel ride to a remote valley for tea with a Bedouin family. Afternoon rest in your bubble with a book, followed by a private candlelit dinner on the dunes under the Milky Way.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Hot-air balloon at dawn
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pre-dawn champagne balloon flight over Wadi Rum — the definitive honeymoon experience in Jordan, drifting above rock arches and Bedouin camps. Afternoon rest and photography from the stargazing dome.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Petra excursion
itinerary_7_nights.description: Early 90-minute drive north to Petra for a guided Siq-to-Treasury visit and Royal Tombs walk. Return to the desert for a private barbeque under a sky so dark the Milky Way casts shadows on the sand.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Canyon hike and hammam
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning hike into the Burrah canyon with a Bedouin guide, afternoon rest and a traditional Arabic hammam treatment at the camp's wellness tent. Final sunset climb to a nearby dune for photos.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final sunrise from the dome, slow breakfast on the terrace and 4x4 transfer out to the Desert Highway for Aqaba airport or the three-hour drive back to Amman.

caveats: Bubble tents are transparent for the star views but run on air-conditioning that keeps them cool in summer and warm in winter — occasional compressor noise is the price of the 360-degree sky.
Wadi Rum is a protected reserve with no mains electricity, patchy mobile signal and limited alcohol service, so honeymooners should treat the stay as an intentional digital and cocktail detox.
Winter nights in the desert can drop below 5C and summer days can exceed 40C, so pack layers and book April, May, October or November for the most comfortable bubble conditions.

faqs.question: Is this hotel good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — Sun City is arguably the most romantic single-night experience in the Middle East. The bubble tents with private stargazing domes, desert silence and zarb dinners under the stars are unbeatable for newlyweds.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: March to early May and late September to November are perfect, with warm 25C days, crisp star-clear nights in the mid-teens and no risk of sandstorms disrupting 4x4 tours or balloon flights.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Reserve a Martian Tent well ahead, a dawn hot-air balloon flight, a private candlelit dinner on the dunes and a 4x4 tour with a knowledgeable English-speaking Bedouin guide for your full day out.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, families do stay, but the remote desert setting, lack of a pool or kids club and the romantic infrastructure of bubble tents and private terraces give the camp a strongly adult, honeymoon-oriented feel.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: The Martian Tent with panoramic stargazing dome — the clear hemisphere above the bed faces the Milky Way and is the single most photographed honeymoon room in Jordan. Avoid the basic Bedouin tents.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into King Hussein International Airport (AQJ) in Aqaba for a one-hour transfer, or into Amman (AMM) for a four-hour drive south. The camp meets guests at Wadi Rum village with 4x4 transfers included.
